title: Enhance toIntervalSecond and add CONVERT_TZ to improve quicksight integration in ClickHouse.
description:
### Company or project name
AWS QuickSight's incremental refresh feature generates MySQL-specific queries that fail in ClickHouse due to missing functions and type support, preventing users from efficiently processing large datasets.
Integration: AWS QuickSight via MySQL driver
Documentation reference: https://clickhouse.com/docs/integrations/quicksight
### Use case
Problem Description
When using QuickSight's incremental refresh capability with ClickHouse, the following query is generated and fails:
```
updated_at > CONVERT_TZ(
fromUnixTimestamp(0) + toIntervalSecond(
CAST(CAST(1753254013359, 'char(10)'), 'decimal')
),
globalVariable('time_zone'),
'UTC'
)
```
Error Message
```
Code: 46. DB::Exception: Unknown function CONVERT_TZ: While processing updated_at > CONVERT_TZ(fromUnixTimestamp(0) + toIntervalSecond(CAST(CAST(1753254013359, 'char(10)'), 'decimal')), globalVariable('time_zone'), 'UTC'). (UNKNOWN_FUNCTION)
```
Root Causes
Missing `CONVERT_TZ` function
Issue: ClickHouse doesn't have MySQL's `CONVERT_TZ` function
Impact: Medium (workaround possible via UDF)
MySQL behaviour: Converts datetime from one timezone to another
`toIntervalSecond` doesn't support decimal types.
Issue: Function only accepts (U)Int*/Float*/String, not Decimal.
Impact: High (no workaround available)
Current supported types: UInt8/16/32/64, Int8/16/32/64, Float32/64, String
QuickSight requirement: Needs Decimal support for timestamp calculations
### Describe the solution you'd like
Add decimal type support to `toIntervalSecond` function.
Add native `CONVERT_TZ` function.
### Describe alternatives you've considered
A UDF implementation:
```
CREATE FUNCTION CONVERT_TZ AS (dt, from_tz, to_tz) ->
toTimeZone(fromTimeZone(dt, from_tz), to_tz);
```
### Additional context
_No response_
poc:
CREATE FUNCTION CONVERT_TZ AS (dt, from_tz, to_tz) -> 
    toTimeZone(fromTimeZone(dt, from_tz), to_tz);
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id UInt32, updated_at DateTime, value Decimal(10, 2)) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY id;
INSERT INTO test_table (id, updated_at, value) VALUES (1, now(), 1753254013.59);
SELECT id, updated_at, toIntervalSecond(CAST(CAST(value, 'char(10)'), 'decimal')) FROM test_table WHERE updated_at > fromUnixTimestamp(0) + toIntervalSecond(0);
CREATE TABLE timezone_table (tz String) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY tz;
